The compounding factor is PinDrop timing out on 34% of degraded-route calls, adding 2:30 to AHT through manual KBA — slower dequeue rate at higher arrival rate. 12 of your agents are idle because their assigned routing paths are dropped; they're not contributing to throughput.

confidence.rationale: Service level math is direct CCAS+Variant telemetry; capacity loss and authentication-fallback contributions are verified against SIP monitor and PinDrop SDK.

intraday_sup_explain_wait_time.ai_message: Average wait time is 12:47 — about 5.9x your 2:10 baseline. The arithmetic: 70% of normal voice capacity carrying 3.2x normal arrival rate. Compounding the queue depth, abandonment is at 18.4%, and CCAS plus IVR re-attempt tracking confirms 34% of today's volume is duplicate-contact retries from members who abandoned earlier — each retry re-enters the queue and waits again. The IVR can't deflect the rate-comparison intent (its current top intent at 67% of inbound), so almost everything is funneling to a live agent.

confidence.rationale: Wait-time arithmetic falls out directly from queue depth + service rate. Retry loop percentage is conservative — ANI-dedup confirms duplicate-contact share.

intraday_sup_explain_handle_time.ai_message: Average handle time is 11:20 — about 2:38 above your 8:42 baseline. Two clean drivers: (1) PinDrop authentication is timing out on 34% of calls on the degraded SIP route. Agents fall back to manual KBA, which adds about 2:30 per call. (2) 67% of today's callers are asking for rate comparisons — the IVR template doesn't render current rate next to promo rate, so agents are doing it conversationally with calculator-assisted math. CRM cross-reference confirms these are existing members in refinance-eligibility mode, not new applicants.

confidence.rationale: Both drivers measurable: PinDrop SDK reports per-call timeout state; CRM has the caller-type split.

intraday_sup_explain_abandonment.ai_message: Abandonment is at 18.4% versus your 6.2% baseline — nearly tripled. CCAS plus IVR ANI-dedup confirms 34% of today's volume is duplicate contacts from the same numbers — each retry re-enters the queue and re-waits, feeding queue depth in a loop that Variant WFM did not model.

confidence.rationale: Abandonment rate is direct CCAS telemetry. The retry-loop percentage is ANI-dedup against the inbound stream — conservative.

intraday_sup_explain_occupancy.ai_message: Agent occupancy is at 97% — past the 95% operational ceiling. At this level coaches can't pull agents for after-call work or scheduled breaks, and adherence starts slipping. The cause is straightforward: Variant WFM forecasted for a 78% baseline, but the schedule is now absorbing 3.2x volume through 70% of voice capacity. 12 agents are idle on the down SIP paths — rerouting them to working paths would relieve about 8 points of occupancy and is approved for one-click activation under Plan A.

confidence.rationale: Occupancy is direct telemetry blend; relief projection draws on the WFM agent-skill matrix and historical reroute outcomes.

intraday_sup_explain_ivr_containment.ai_message: IVR containment is at 22% — down from your 41% baseline. The drop is concentrated in rate-comparison intent. The current IVR flow can quote a promo rate but can't show the member's current rate alongside it, which is what 67% of today's callers actually want. The AI intent classifier confirms 'compare my current rate' is today's top unresolved self-service intent at 0.91 precision. There's a pre-built template in your IVR Flow Manager library that handles side-by-side comparison — deploying it would lift containment an estimated 12-16 percentage points.
